rationale: The cited paper’s core finding is that consumers can systematically choose non-cost-minimizing, higher-cost options (“paying too much and being happy about it”), which is conceptually close to the citing paper’s mechanism of shifting shoppers toward higher-priced purchases via search frictions and price-sensitivity sorting. Given this likely behavioral/theoretical relevance and no evidence that it is merely a perfunctory mention (only a DOI match is available), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
